summary: Complete testing of Discogs Collection Import feature. Backend: 27/27 tests passed covering status, connect-token, import, progress, summary, and disconnect endpoints. Frontend: DiscogsImport card verified on Collection page with connected status, sync button, last synced date, summary modal with stats grid, sample covers, and collection value display.
passed_tests: GET /api/discogs/status returns 200 with connection info (connected, discogs_username, last_synced, connected_at)
GET /api/discogs/status requires authentication
POST /api/discogs/connect-token with valid username returns 200 and connects
POST /api/discogs/connect-token with invalid username returns 400 'not found'
POST /api/discogs/connect-token requires authentication
GET /api/discogs/status shows connected=true after connect
GET /api/discogs/import/progress returns status, total, imported, skipped, errors, sample_covers
GET /api/discogs/import/progress requires authentication
DELETE /api/discogs/disconnect removes connection successfully
DELETE /api/discogs/disconnect requires authentication
POST /api/discogs/import fails with 400 when not connected
POST /api/discogs/import starts when connected
GET /api/records shows imported records (143+ with discogs_id)
GET /api/discogs/import/summary returns has_import, imported, skipped, total, sample_covers, collection_stats
GET /api/discogs/import/summary.collection_stats includes total_records, total_value, valued_count
GET /api/platform-fee returns 200 without auth (public endpoint)
GET /api/platform-fee returns platform_fee_percent field (6%)
Sync with all duplicates shows skipped=143 (duplicate handling working)
DiscogsImport card shows on Collection page with data-testid='discogs-import-card'
DiscogsImport card shows 'Connected as katieintheafterglow'
DiscogsImport card shows 'Collection in sync — 143 records already imported'
View Summary button opens modal with stats grid (0 imported, 143 skipped, 143 total)
Summary modal shows collection value card (163 records, ~$7,603 estimated)
Summary modal 'Got it' button dismisses modal
Sync Now button present with last synced date
Disconnect button present for connected users
ISOPage.js fetches dynamic platform fee from /api/platform-fee (line 58-59)
seed_data_creation: None - used existing demo user with Discogs connection to 'katieintheafterglow' (143 records)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Discogs Import feature fully tested and working. Demo user has Discogs connected as 'katieintheafterglow' with 143 records synced (all duplicates on re-sync). Collection page shows DiscogsImport card with status, sync button, View Summary modal. Summary modal fetches from /api/discogs/import/summary and shows stats grid (imported/skipped/total), sample covers grid, and collection value card. ISOPage.js uses dynamic platform fee from /api/platform-fee instead of hardcoded 6%. OnboardingModal has 'Import from Discogs' button at Step 1 with username input, progress bar, and completion message - only shows for users who haven't completed onboarding.

discogs_connect_token_endpoint.error_messages: Returns 400 with specific message for invalid users or private collections

discogs_import_endpoint.duplicate_handling: Skips records with same discogs_id already in user's collection

onboarding_discogs_import.note: Only shows for users who haven't completed onboarding

iso_page_dynamic_fee.usage: Displayed in Make Offer modal: '{platformFee}% platform fee applies'

mocked_api_note: None - all Discogs endpoints are real. Discogs market value fetching runs as background task after import (_post_import_value_refresh).
